title: Festuca hedgei
taxonomy: Festuca hedgei is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified as part of the genus Festuca, which includes many species of fescue grasses. Festuca hedgei is often found in alpine and subalpine regions, where it grows in rocky outcrops and meadows. The plant is native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. It is a cold-tolerant, perennial grass species with a narrow range of distribution.

intro: Festuca hedgei, also known as hedge bluegrass or mountain fescue, is a perennial grass species native to mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Festuca hedgei include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Festuca hedgei is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and extracts, which are used to support digestive health and immune function.
